Na boca, é seco, com ótima acidez, muito equilibrado, corpo médio, taninos equilibrados, álcool equilibrado, moderadamente intenso e persistente, com final fresco.

style.name: Bordeaux Pomerol
style.description:
Bordeaux Pomerol wines are rich, decadent reds, with notes of chocolate, dark cherry, plums and spice, soft tannins and elegance courtesy of the Merlot grape.

The most lush—and expensive—wines come from a plateau of clay, gravel and iron, while lighter, more acidic wines come from the region's sandy soils.

Situated on the Right Bank of Bordeaux, Pomerol is dominated by small estates that command premium prices.

Approximately 80 percent of the region's plantings are Merlot, unlike other regions of Bordeaux where no one grape dominates so singularly; and one disadvantage is that bad weather can wreak havoc on an individual vintage.
interesting_facts: Pomerol is the smallest of Bordeaux's major appellations.
Pomerol is the only major Bordeaux appellation that has not been classified.
Cabernet Franc was the dominant grape of Pomerol until phylloxera hit, at which point it became out-planted by Merlot, the grape that now dominates the region.
The region of Pomerol claims the three most expensive wines produced in Bordeaux: Château Pétrus, Château Le Pin and Château Lafleur.
The wines of Château Pétrus are planted on unique blue clay soils, which are thought to be approximately 40 million years old and the best terroir in Pomerol.

reviews.note: Otimo vinho de Pomerol! Intenso, tanino equilibrado e uma grande experiencia!

style.name: Brunello italiano
style.description:
From the picturesque hilltop town of Montalcino in Tuscany, Brunello is one of Italy's best red wines.

Brunello is made totally from Sangiovese, Tuscany's flagship grape that is prized for its ability to reflect where it's grown. It is typically bolder than Chianti, Tuscany's other famous Sangiovese wine. Why not find out for yourself by trying Brunello and Chianti side-by-side? 

This wine pairs well with big protein laden dishes. You may also enjoy it with salted cheeses and cured meats. Try to avoid pairing with dishes where the wine would overwhelm flavors, such as leafy greens and subtly seasoned meats.

Thanks to its high acidity, Brunello ages really well, which makes it great for collecting.
style.blurb: Vinho DOCG da Toscana unicamente com Sangiovese
interesting_facts: Brunello comes from a particular Sangiovese grape - the same grape from which Chianti is made.
Brunello must be aged for 4 years before release, 2 of which must be in barrel.
In 1980, Brunello was one of the first Italian wine regions to receive the country's top classification of Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG).

reviews.note: Delicado e harmonioso, um toque de baunilha, um ótimo corpo e taninos redondos. Quente e muito persistente. Um excelente Brunello.
reviews.tagged_note: Delicado e harmonioso, um toque de baunilha, um ótimo corpo e taninos redondos. Quente e muito persistente. Um excelente Brunello.

style.name: Alentejano tinto português
style.description:
The Alentejo region covers approximately a third of Portugal, producing red wines that are easy-drinking and fruit-forward.

Aragonez is the most widely-planted red grape in the region, which produces full-bodied, budget friendly red wines. Castelao, Trincadeira are also widely planted. Frequently, wines from this region are blends of several grapes.

Although traditional Portuguese grape varieties dominate the region, many red wines from Alentejo also incorporate international grape varieties such as Syrah and Cabernet Sauvignon.

The region produces ripe, full bodied and fruit forward wines that are a product of the hot, inland region. The wines are often described as “New World” in style.

Due to the reliable climate, wines from Alentejo combine quality with affordability, offering great bang for the buck. It offers some of the best value and quality for the money of any European wine region.

Wine from this region pairs excellently with hearty, full-bodied dishes.
interesting_facts: Aragonez, the most widely planted grape in Alentejo region, is also known as Tempranillo.
Vineyard land in Alentejo is among the most expensive in all of Portugal.
The region of Alentejo is noted for its extensive cork production.

reviews.note: Dos vinhos mais equlibrados e elegantes que provei!Extremamente bem feito com aromas fruta vermelha, madeira bem integrada, corpo e acidez no ponto.Final elegante médio.O melhor que provei do Paulo Laureano.

winery.name: Quinta Nova de Nossa Senhora do Carmo

style.name: Douro tinto português
style.description:
Douro Red wines range in style from light and fruity to lush and smooth to dark and dense with flavors of mature dark fruit, with herbal and schist.

The wines are often and inky purple due to the thickening of the skins from the intense summer heat of the region.

Most of the best made reds from the region come from the larger port houses where the skill of the winemakers go back for generations. The wines tend to be an incredible value for the time being but costs are slowly increasing due to the amazing blends coming from the area.

The first time one sees the Douro region they will always ask themselves, how can wine be made in such rugged terrain. It is also an amazingly beautiful area, with hillsides covered in terraced vineyards. Most larger winemakers have also started converting their areas into tourist destinations and wine hotels.

Grapes include the Touriga Francesa, Tinta Roriz, Touriga Nacional and Tinta Barroca. Older vineyards still contain many less used vine varieties which makes their annual efforts less predictable, but allowing for unexpected surprises yielding unique, complex wines.
interesting_facts: The Douro Valley is the home of Port wine.
There is evidence as early as the 3rd century AD of winegrowing in the region.
Temperatures in the Douro Valley often reach above 100 degrees F in the summer months. This lends to the inky color of the red wines, whose skin is thicker than cool climate grapes.

reviews.note: Certamente um dos melhores tintos do Douro. O vinho tem uma história e tanto. Entre 1979 e 1981 o Ministério da Agricultura de Portugal e a vinícola Quinta Nova se associaram para estudar as castas do Douro. A parcela 28 com a Tinta Roriz e a parcela 21 com castas antigas indecifráveis; resultado um tinto único com corpo cheio, sedoso e austero. Aromas de frutos azuis e mineralidade. Retrogosto persistente e elegante. SC: 97/100

style.name: Rioja espanhol tinto
style.description:
Rioja is one Spain's most famous wine regions and is one of only two regions classified under the Denominación de Origen Calificada (DOCa) system. Its two most important red grapes are Tempranillo and Garnacha. 

When it comes to Rioja reds, there are 4 categories of ageing to keep in mind:

Joven - There are no specific requirements here, but these wines tend to be 1-2 years old. 

Crianza - These wines are in their 3rd year, with at least 12 months spent in oak barrels.

Reserva - When excellent vintages are selected to become Reserva wines, they spend 3 years aging, 12 months minimum of which is in oak, followed by 6 months in the bottle. 

Gran Reserva - The wines carefully selected for this process must undergo a minimum of 60 months aging,  of which 2 years must be in oak barrels, with another 2 years spent in the bottle. 

Typically younger wines will be lighter in body style, exhibit more fresh fruit characteristics and are suitable to drink while young. Older wines have more developed flavor profiles and earthy characteristics.
interesting_facts: Archaeologists believe that wine has been cultivated and consumed in the region since between 3000 and 4000 BC.
In Spain, wineries are most commonly known as 'bodegas'.
The medium body and high acidity in Rioja Reds make them excellent for pairing with a variety of foods.

style.name: Brunello italiano
style.description:
From the picturesque hilltop town of Montalcino in Tuscany, Brunello is one of Italy's best red wines.

Brunello is made totally from Sangiovese, Tuscany's flagship grape that is prized for its ability to reflect where it's grown. It is typically bolder than Chianti, Tuscany's other famous Sangiovese wine. Why not find out for yourself by trying Brunello and Chianti side-by-side? 

This wine pairs well with big protein laden dishes. You may also enjoy it with salted cheeses and cured meats. Try to avoid pairing with dishes where the wine would overwhelm flavors, such as leafy greens and subtly seasoned meats.

Thanks to its high acidity, Brunello ages really well, which makes it great for collecting.
style.blurb: Vinho DOCG da Toscana unicamente com Sangiovese
interesting_facts: Brunello comes from a particular Sangiovese grape - the same grape from which Chianti is made.
Brunello must be aged for 4 years before release, 2 of which must be in barrel.
In 1980, Brunello was one of the first Italian wine regions to receive the country's top classification of Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG).

wines.name: La Linterna Finca El Tomillo Parcela #1 Gualtallary Chardonnay

style.name: Chardonnay argentino
style.description:
Argentinian Chardonnay wines are some of the most popular white wines in the country, but they are quickly gaining a reputation for being a stand-out region worldwide as well. There has been something of a revolution in the last few decades to seek out new and unique places to grow the grape, including extreme locations in the mountains.

Argentina is blessed with altitude and sunshine, 2 factors that can really make this grape pop. If you look at wines composed of grapes grown at higher altitude in the mountains, you'll find delicious citrus and mineral characters. A bit further down in the sunny valleys, more lush stone fruits will show up in the glass. 

Argentinian Chardonnay will pair well with buttery sauces, complement poultry and fish dishes, and go great with mild, fresh cheese.
interesting_facts: Argentina was the first country to plant Chardonnay grapes at over 1000 meters above sea level. You would need to stack almost 4 Eiffel Towers to reach that height.

reviews.note:

style.name: Rioja espanhol tinto
style.description:
Rioja is one Spain's most famous wine regions and is one of only two regions classified under the Denominación de Origen Calificada (DOCa) system. Its two most important red grapes are Tempranillo and Garnacha. 

When it comes to Rioja reds, there are 4 categories of ageing to keep in mind:

Joven - There are no specific requirements here, but these wines tend to be 1-2 years old. 

Crianza - These wines are in their 3rd year, with at least 12 months spent in oak barrels.

Reserva - When excellent vintages are selected to become Reserva wines, they spend 3 years aging, 12 months minimum of which is in oak, followed by 6 months in the bottle. 

Gran Reserva - The wines carefully selected for this process must undergo a minimum of 60 months aging,  of which 2 years must be in oak barrels, with another 2 years spent in the bottle. 

Typically younger wines will be lighter in body style, exhibit more fresh fruit characteristics and are suitable to drink while young. Older wines have more developed flavor profiles and earthy characteristics.
interesting_facts: Archaeologists believe that wine has been cultivated and consumed in the region since between 3000 and 4000 BC.
In Spain, wineries are most commonly known as 'bodegas'.
The medium body and high acidity in Rioja Reds make them excellent for pairing with a variety of foods.

Localizada em San Vicente de la Sonsierra,fundada em 2000 c/ 15 hectares que produzem ao redor de 8000 garrafas/ano,Heredad de San Andrés é um achado de Rioja.Cheio de personalidade,boa dose de elegância e certa complexidade,alterna nuances frutadas de cereja negra,amora,ameixas maduras,chocolate ao leite,c/ricas notas de barrica(28 meses em carvalho francês),tostados de especiarias,caramelo.Robusto e envolvente na boca,textura macia,taninos de fina granulação,acidez equilibrada e final mediano.

Ótimo!

style.name: Douro tinto português
style.description:
Douro Red wines range in style from light and fruity to lush and smooth to dark and dense with flavors of mature dark fruit, with herbal and schist.

The wines are often and inky purple due to the thickening of the skins from the intense summer heat of the region.

Most of the best made reds from the region come from the larger port houses where the skill of the winemakers go back for generations. The wines tend to be an incredible value for the time being but costs are slowly increasing due to the amazing blends coming from the area.

The first time one sees the Douro region they will always ask themselves, how can wine be made in such rugged terrain. It is also an amazingly beautiful area, with hillsides covered in terraced vineyards. Most larger winemakers have also started converting their areas into tourist destinations and wine hotels.

Grapes include the Touriga Francesa, Tinta Roriz, Touriga Nacional and Tinta Barroca. Older vineyards still contain many less used vine varieties which makes their annual efforts less predictable, but allowing for unexpected surprises yielding unique, complex wines.
interesting_facts: The Douro Valley is the home of Port wine.
There is evidence as early as the 3rd century AD of winegrowing in the region.
Temperatures in the Douro Valley often reach above 100 degrees F in the summer months. This lends to the inky color of the red wines, whose skin is thicker than cool climate grapes.

style.name: Cabernet Sauvignon australiano
style.description:
While the birthplace of Cabernet Sauvignon is in Bordeaux, Australia has found great success growing it, especially in the regions of Coonawarra and Margaret River.
Here, the classic Cabernet taste of dark, rich fruit, cedar, and mint is accompanied by flavors of eucalyptus. 

The Cabs of Australia possess a beautiful balance of fruit, acidity and tannin. With all of these elements in harmony, these wines are delightful after a few years of aging. 

A food lover’s best friend, take these fantastic wines to the next level by pairing them with delicious dishes. Try them with different kinds of game, hard cheeses or a slow-cooked ragu.
interesting_facts: Some of the oldest Cabernet Sauvignon vines in the world can be found in Australia.
Cabernet Sauvignon is the grape-child of the red grape Cabernet Franc and the green grape Sauvignon Blanc.

reviews.note: Para o gran finale, este magnífico Cab australiano 2013 com uma tábua de frios feito pelas mãos da competente Rebbeca.Vamos ruminar este líquido precioso como um alce.

style.name: Cabernet Sauvignon argentino
style.description:
If you like rich, full wines with dark fruit flavors, then you might want to try the fruit-forward Cabernet Sauvignons from Argentina. 

The dry, hot climate of Argentina is really great for growing Cabernet Sauvignon. The grapes ripen very easily, so these Cabs are able to show a wonderful depth of flavor, with lots of blackberries, cherry, pepper, vanilla and with a little bit of mint. 

Eat it like a local and pair with a classic beef empanada, a delicious meat-filled pastry served with parsley-packed chimichurri sauce.
interesting_facts: Cabernet Sauvignon accounts for just over 17% of the red grapes grown in Argentina.
Some of the highest altitude vineyards in the world are from Argentina, and some of the best of these sky-high wines are composed of Cabernet Sauvignon.
